channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jackson outduels Daniels and Henry runs for two TDs as Ravens beat Commanders 30-23
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson threw nine passes to Zay Flowers in the first half and never went to him again. Mark Andrews rebounded from a ball bouncing off his hands for an interception to catch his first touchdown pass of the season. And Derrick Henry was Derrick Henry.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: England gets back to winning ways but interim coach Carsley says team needs a 'world class' manager
item.description: After three wins from four games as England interim coach, Lee Carsley appeared to distance himself from taking on the job full time. A 3-1 victory against Finland in the Nations League on Sunday saw England get back to winning ways after the shock 2-1 home loss to Greece.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Winners of the 2024 Bank of America Chicago Marathon
item.description: Runners from across the world gathered in Chicago Sunday for the 2024 Bank of America Chicago Marathon.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Chargers' Jim Harbaugh abruptly leaves game with mystery illness, returns minutes later
item.description: Los Angeles Chargers head coach Jim Harbaugh left Sunday's game against the Denver Broncos for a few minutes with an undisclosed illness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Billy Crystal and Spike Lee take their places at the Hall of Fame as basketball superfans
item.description: Honored for his devotion to a basketball team that doesn’t have a Hall of Fame history, Billy Crystal couldn’t help but note the irony.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mahoney, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex-NFL star questions why no flag after Saints wide receiver Chris Olave takes huge hit, knocked out of game
item.description: New Orleans Saints wide receiver Chris Olave was concussed on a play against the Tampa Bay Buccaneers and a former star wondered where the flag was.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jets' Jeff Ulbrich once thought being a coach was 'craziness' until one play made it all make sense
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (AP) — Jeff Ulbrich's passion for coaching began with a play by the man who replaced him on the football field.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960. Texas strengthened its hold on No.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Zealand opens up 3-0 lead over Britain in America’s Cup final after almost crashing
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Emirates Team New Zealand avoided a crash and nailed INEOS Britannia with a penalty as it opened up a 3-0 lead in the first-to-seven wins finals of the America’s Cup on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Williams throws 4 TDs and TE Kmet handles long snaps for Bears in 35-16 rout of Jaguars in London
item.description: Caleb Williams threw four touchdown passes — two each to receiver Keenan Allen and tight end Cole Kmet — and the Chicago Bears held a tea party celebration in the end zone in a 35-16 rout of the Jacksonville Jaguars at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London.
item.dc:creator: Ken Maguire, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jannik Sinner beats Novak Djokovic to take Shanghai Masters title, Sabalenka reigns in Wuhan
item.description: SHANGHAI (AP) — Top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Novak Djokovic in straight sets to win the Shanghai Masters on Sunday, giving the Italian his tour-leading seventh title of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bears' Caleb Williams inexplicably throws pass off teammate's facemask
item.description: Chicago Bears rookie quarterback Caleb Williams made a head-scratching play in the first quarter against the Jacksonville Jaguars on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sharks takeaways: No Celebrini, but improvement. Now, ‘we’ve just got to learn how to win’
item.description: The San Jose Sharks are a much better team right now than they were last year at this time. Now they need a win.
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: 49ers await Super Bowl rematch with Chiefs — without trophy at stake
item.description: The 49ers (3-3) look to carry momentum from Thursday’s win at Seattle into their first win over the Patrick Mahomes-led Chiefs next Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Johnson shrugs off late interception, throws winning TD as No. 18 Kansas St beats Colorado 31-28
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (AP) — Avery Johnson rebounded from throwing a late interception by finding Jayce Brown for a 50-yard touchdown with 2:14 remaining as No. 18 Kansas State escaped with a 31-28 win over Colorado on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado two-way star Travis Hunter leaves game against Kansas State with apparent shoulder injury
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (AP) — Colorado two-way star Travis Hunter left the Buffaloes' 31-28 loss to No. 18 Kansas State late in the second quarter Saturday night after a right shoulder injury on a 14-yard reception. Three other Buffs receivers were injured in the game: speedster Jimmy Horn Jr.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nussmeier throws late TD passes, No. 13 LSU rallies to beat No. 9 Ole Miss 29-26 in overtime
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Garrett Nussmeier was in the midst of what the LSU quarterback considered one of the worst games of his career. His last two snaps changed everything.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senga's comeback to continue with start for Mets in Game 1 of NLCS vs Dodgers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mets pitcher Kodai Senga will start Game 1 of the National League Championship Series against Jack Flaherty and the Los Angeles Dodgers on Sunday, continuing his comeback from injuries that have limited him to two starts this year.
item.dc:creator: JERRY BEACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Padres vs. Dodgers Game 5: Who will advance to the NLCS?
item.description: PHOTOS: The San Diego Padres and Los Angeles Dodgers will face off in Game 5 of the National League Division Series (NLDS) on Friday, Oct. 11 at Dodger Stadium. The winner of this game will advance to the NLCS, which starts on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: How to cheer on the Padres for NLDS Game 5
item.description: Fans are still cheering on the San Diego Padres as they take on the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 of the National League Division Series Friday evening, following Wednesday's 8-0 blowout.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Cam Rising to start tomorrow at Arizona State
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It looks like Cam Rising is ready to return to the field. According to a report by ActionNetwork's Brett McMurphy, Rising will start at quarterback for the #16 Utah football team tomorrow night at Arizona State.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU looks to pick up where it left off after bye week
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It's not ideal to have a bye week while on a five-game winning streak, but #14 BYU needed the rest after getting off to a 5-0 start.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: PGA Golfers impressed with Black Desert Resort
item.description: IVINS, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The PGA Tour returns to Utah for the first time since 1963 when the Black Desert Championship tees off Thursday in Ivins, Utah. Most of the 132 golfers in the tournament have never played in Utah before, and they have been impressed with the course at Black Desert Resort.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ahead of team's game in London, Bears president says he remains steadfast on proceeding with plans for new lakefront stadium
item.description: WARE, England (AP) — The Bears remain focused on the city’s lakefront as the location for a nearly $5 billion stadium development project, team president and CEO Kevin Warren reiterated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC players understand significance of first NHL game
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - No matter what happens this season, there will never be another first NHL game for the Utah Hockey Club It's just game 1 of 82, but the Utah HC players know how historic tonight's game against the Chicago Blackhawks at the Delta Center will be.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets fire head coach Robert Saleh after a 2-3 start to the season, AP source says
item.description: The New York Jets have fired head coach Robert Saleh after the team's 2-3 start in his fourth season, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press. Saleh was 20-36 as coach of the Jets, who are trying to snap the NFL's longest active playoff drought at 13 seasons.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bedard, Blackhawks embark on 2024-25 season Tuesday night in Utah
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Connor Bedard was one of the first players on the ice Monday. He was also one of the last to leave when practice was over. In many ways, Bedard is the same guy as he begins his second season with the Chicago Blackhawks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift is back to watch Travis Kelce and the Chiefs with Eras Tour soon to resume
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Taylor Swift was back at Arrowhead Stadium on Monday night to see her boyfriend, Travis Kelce, and the Kansas City Chiefs play the New Orleans Saints after the pop superstar had missed the team's previous two games on the road.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: Padres look to rebound in game 2 of NLDS against Dodgers
item.description: Photos: The San Diego Padres are facing off against the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 2 of the NL Division Series on Sunday, with Yu Darvish starting for the Padres.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5
